tags.note: The Slip (70) located where Middle Road parallels the Parker River. The remains Of early pilings can be seen.

tags.note: For kayaks, canoes, small skiffs. Electric motors only.

name: Blessing Of The Bay Boat Ramp

tags.name: Blessing of the Bay Boat Ramp
Newbury Landing
tags.note: Marston's Wharf (19) located on the north bank of the Parker River at High Road. Fred "Shorty" Marston. called Shorty because he was very tall, ran a sea food restaurant here. The restaurant was torn down in the 1930s.
tags.source:name: http://mvpc.org/wp-content/uploads/Newbury-Town-Landing.pdf

tags.description: A very steep slipway. It is impossible to back a boat and car straight down it due to car barriers about 15ft in front of it. Good for kayaks, paddle boards and small boats that can be wheeled down by hand. Free to use.

tags.canoe:description: Private canoe and kayak access to Quabbin Reservoir is not allowed due to concerns with the introduction of aquatic invasive species.

tags.description: Boathouse and launching rails of the former Dolliver's Neck Coast Guard Station. The Coast Guard Station relocated to its current site in 1974.

tags.attribution: Office of Geographic and Environmental Information (MassGIS)
